title: Standard-Name review-batch manifest
description: A frozen, provenance-carrying list of standard-name ids that constitutes one review batch. This is the review/approval currency: export, PR, merge, and approval all operate on the SN ids listed here. Minted live from an sn-sources file (path -> name join + immediate-family closure) and materialised so the reviewed set is reproducible and can be approved exactly on merge.

kind.description: File-kind discriminator. Must be 'sn_names' — the release-side --focus dispatcher validates against this schema when it sees this value.

schema_version.description: Manifest format version. Loaders reject unknown versions.

name.description: Stable kebab-case identifier for this batch.

description.description: One-paragraph synopsis of what the batch covers.

names.description: The batch's standard-name ids. This is the authoritative reviewed set carried through export -> PR -> merge.

minted_from.description: The sn-sources file the batch was minted from (provenance).

rc_version.description: The self-assigned RC catalog version that carries this batch (e.g. 'v0.2.0rc65'). Known before push, so it is the traceable key.

minted_at.description: ISO timestamp the batch was minted.

pr_number.description: Upstream PR number, back-filled after gh pr create.

pr_url.description: Upstream PR URL, back-filled after gh pr create.

merge_commit.description: Merge-commit SHA, back-filled after the PR is merged.

unmatched_sources.description: Informational: DD source paths from minted_from that had no linked live standard name (reported, not dropped).
